report_name: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Affecting Langflow and Trend Micro to Catalog

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Threat Actors
threat_actors.description: Unspecified threat actors confirmed by CISA to be actively exploiting vulnerabilities CVE-2025-34291 and CVE-2026-34926 in the wild.
threat_actors.goals: Compromise vulnerable systems by exploiting flaws in Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Actively exploiting an Origin Validation Error (CVE-2025-34291) in Langflow and a Directory Traversal vulnerability (CVE-2026-34926) in on-premise Trend Micro Apex One to compromise systems.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2025-34291
vulnerabilities.description: An Origin Validation Error in Langflow that is under active exploitation. This flaw can lead to security bypasses, allowing an attacker to execute unauthorized actions, steal sensitive data, or perform cross-site scripting attacks.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-34926
vulnerabilities.description: A Directory Traversal vulnerability in on-premise instances of Trend Micro Apex One that is under active exploitation. This flaw allows an attacker to access sensitive files outside the web root folder by manipulating file path variables, which could lead to information disclosure or arbitrary code execution.

identities.name: Federal Civilian Executive Branch (FCEB) agencies
identities.description: U.S. federal agencies mandated by CISA's Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 22-01 to remediate the actively exploited vulnerabilities in Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One.

indicators.name: Potential Directory Traversal Attempt in URL
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Directory Traversal Attempt in URL
id: 2b8a1c9d-5e6f-4a3b-8c7d-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects URL patterns indicative of directory traversal attempts, such as those related to the exploitation of CVE-2026-34926 in Trend Micro Apex One.
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/2026/05/21/cisa-adds-two-known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-22
tags:
  - attack.t1190
level: high
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - '../'
      - '..%2f'
      - '..\\' # Escaped for YAML
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects web requests containing common directory traversal sequences in the URL, which could indicate attempts to exploit vulnerabilities like CVE-2026-34926.

relationships.source_name: Potential Directory Traversal Attempt in URL
